what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note in the dragnet-development channel that records Tim's agreement with an arc-shaped radial menu over a full circle, including reasoning about thumb occlusion, one-handed phone grip, and corner anchoring.
observations: This note was authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel and comments on board://item-d6cdf466.
It explicitly accepts the expert recommendation to use an arc rather than a full radial menu to avoid hand occlusion.
It proposes quarter/half arcs anchored in screen corners, opening up and inward, because the user holds the phone one-handed.
It notes four corners as natural homes for radial menu items and argues an arc is less dominating than a full circle.
It allows that a full circle may still be useful when centered on the screen for a main system action.
The body references a touchscreen-game radial thread and Tim's own words in BD-G.
No code symbols are present in the file.
standouts: Clear acceptance of arc-over-full-circle UX finding
Thumb occlusion and one-handed grip as the central rationale
Corner-anchored arc as a less dominating, four-corners-compatible pattern
conventions: Follows the noticeboard YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history
Uses a [point] tag in the Markdown body to label the note's role
References another board item via a commented_on link
Conversational, first-person tone rather than a formal decision record
concerns: Informal phrasing and typos (e.g., 'ark') may reduce precision for later retrieval
The cited source thread and BD-G reference are not linked or identified concretely
The exception for centered full circles is stated as speculation rather than validated
notes: Tim's note accepting an arc-shaped radial menu for one-handed mobile use, anchored in a corner to avoid thumb occlusion, with a caveat that centered system menus might still use a full circle.
questions: What specific design artifact or prototype does this feedback apply to?
What does board://item-d6cdf466 contain, and how does it relate to this point?
What is 'BD-G' and where can Tim's earlier statement be found?
Is the four-corners arc concept already represented in a mockup or specification?
How will the exception for centered main-system menus be resolved?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is labeled as a note/point and does in fact record a single point of feedback responding to another board item.
apparent_themes: Corner anchoring and screen real estate
intention_signals: To capture Tim's agreement with a UX finding, explain why an arc fits the one-handed grip and corner model, and flag a boundary condition where a full circle might still be appropriate.
novelty: It blends cited touchscreen-game radial-menu wisdom with a four-corners mental model and a nuanced exception for centered system menus.
contribution: A stakeholder's explicit acceptance of arc-over-circle radial menus, a rationale grounded in thumb occlusion and one-handed grip, a corner-anchoring concept, and a documented exception for centered main-system menus.
summary_for_embedding: A posted noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development agreeing that radial menus should use a corner-anchored arc to avoid thumb occlusion, while noting that full circles may still suit centered system menus.
intention_for_embedding: To record Tim's UX feedback accepting an arc-shaped radial menu and explain the occlusion-based reasoning plus an exception for centered main actions.
